title: Can't run container with systemd rootless on RockyLinux 8.6
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Trying to run the container `quay.io/freeipa/freeipa-server:almalinux-9-4.10.0` (or rocky version) which includes systemd interactively appears to work as root, but not rootless.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create RockyLinux8.6 based system.
1. Disable SELinux (I have no idea if that is required, it is just my current configuration)
1. As a non-root user run:

        podman run -ti --replace --name freeipa-server --read-only --dns=127.0.0.1 --hostname ipa01.noetchosts.test -e IPA_SERVER_IP=10.60.102.176 -v /var/lib/state/ipa-data:/data:Z quay.io/freeipa/freeipa-server:almalinux-9-4.10.0 --no-ntp


Note that running the same command as root suceeeds.



**Describe the results you received:**

```
62997550efcf76787b0d5b4e35e2705f0d84848c8e45bc1a979e1218b4a2ca72
systemd 250-12.el9_1 running in system mode (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX -APPARMOR +IMA +SMACK +SECCOMP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +OPENSSL +ACL +BLKID +CURL +ELFUTILS -FIDO2 +IDN2 -IDN -IPTC +KMOD +LIBCRYPTSETUP +LIBFDISK +PCRE2 -PWQUALITY +P11KIT -QRENCODE +BZIP2 +LZ4 +XZ +ZLIB +ZSTD -BPF_FRAMEWORK +XKBCOMMON +UTMP +SYSVINIT default-hierarchy=unified)
Detected virtualization container-other.
Detected architecture x86-64.
Failed to create /user.slice/user-1000.slice/session-7.scope/init.scope control group: Permission denied
Failed to allocate manager object: Permission denied
[!!!!!!] Failed to allocate manager object.
Exiting PID 1...
```


**Describe the results you expected:**
As root, looks like this:
```
systemd 250-12.el9_1 running in system mode (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX -APPARMOR +IMA +SMACK +SECCOMP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +OPENSSL +ACL +BLKID +CURL +ELFUTILS -FIDO2 +IDN2 -IDN -IPTC +KMOD +LIBCRYPTSETUP +LIBFDISK +PCRE2 -PWQUALITY +P11KIT -QRENCODE +BZIP2 +LZ4 +XZ +ZLIB +ZSTD -BPF_FRAMEWORK +XKBCOMMON +UTMP +SYSVINIT default-hierarchy=unified)
Detected virtualization container-other.
Detected architecture x86-64.
Queued start job for default target Minimal target for containerized FreeIPA server.
systemd-journald.service: unit configures an IP firewall, but the local system does not support BPF/cgroup firewalling.
(This warning is only shown for the first unit using IP firewalling.)
Wed Nov 23 10:58:39 UTC 2022 /usr/sbin/ipa-server-configure-first 

The log file for this installation can be found in /var/log/ipaserver-install.log
==============================================================================
This program will set up the IPA Server.
Version 4.10.0
<snip>
```
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.17.12
Built:        Tue Oct 25 15:55:58 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
# rpm -q podman
podman-4.1.1-7.module+el8.6.0+1054+50b00ff4.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes - this is the latest available on RL8.6

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
OpenStack.

comments.body: Meant to say, I think this looks like https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7441 but that should not apply to podman v4.
Have you tried forcing systemd mode with `--systemd=always`?

Same result:
```
[podman@noetchosts-control ~]$ podman run -ti --systemd=always --replace --name freeipa-server --read-only --dns=127.0.0.1 --hostname ipa01.noetchosts.test -e IPA_SERVER_IP=10.60.102.176 -v /var/lib/state/ipa-data:/data:Z quay.io/freeipa/freeipa-server:almalinux-9-4.10.0 --no-ntp
4981573b2a1a9dec72b5f248d2ec96b34cfd3068444127dc326789658c61e7d3
systemd 250-12.el9_1 running in system mode (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX -APPARMOR +IMA +SMACK +SECCOMP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +OPENSSL +ACL +BLKID +CURL +ELFUTILS -FIDO2 +IDN2 -IDN -IPTC +KMOD +LIBCRYPTSETUP +LIBFDISK +PCRE2 -PWQUALITY +P11KIT -QRENCODE +BZIP2 +LZ4 +XZ +ZLIB +ZSTD -BPF_FRAMEWORK +XKBCOMMON +UTMP +SYSVINIT default-hierarchy=unified)
Detected virtualization container-other.
Detected architecture x86-64.
Failed to create /user.slice/user-1000.slice/session-7.scope/init.scope control group: Permission denied
Failed to allocate manager object: Permission denied
[!!!!!!] Failed to allocate manager object.
Exiting PID 1...
```
Hm. @giuseppe Rootless systemd containers should work on CGv1, right? That's the only thing that sticks out to me here.

As I understand, thas cgroups v2 anyway?
```
[podman@noetchosts-control ~]$ grep cgroup /proc/filesystems
nodev   cgroup
nodev   cgroup2
```

When both v1 and v2 are mounted (hybrid), we only use v1. You can see in `podman info`:
```
  cgroupVersion: v1
```
We only work with rootless mode on V2.  Period.  Reboot with V2.

When you say:
> We only work with rootless mode on V2

Do you mean _systemd_ only works with rootless mode on v2? Because I've got rootless containers (without systemd) running on a machine which `podman info --debug` shows as being on v1.

Is this documented anywhere? If not, could it be? ETA: or the error message made clearer?
